{{Infobox group
| name = Wei clan
| introduced = cradle-01-unsouled
}}

''See also: [[Wei clan Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|The Wei clan is a prominent faction located in [[Sacred Valley]], characterized by its use of light and dream-based [[madra]] to deceive opponents. This reliance on illusion is central to their identity, as they prioritize deception in service of honor. Their clan symbol is a white fox, and their architecture frequently utilizes purple and white colors, inspired by orus trees and snowfoxes. A notable landmark in their territory is a massive white stone tower that serves as a prominent feature of the valley. The clan's history is tied to [[Elder Whisper]], a founder who established the [[Path of the White Fox]] and used it to assimilate smaller groups. Leadership is held by a Patriarch, such as [[Wei Jin Sairus]], though day-to-day operations are managed by elders. The clan maintains strict traditions, including communal morning meditation and cycling sessions. They are also known for hosting the [[Seven-Year Festival]], a period of intense preparation and presentation. Members of the clan include various high-ranking individuals like Wei Shi [[Kelsa]], a powerful [[Lowgold]] artist, and [[Wei Shi Jaran]]. The clan has experienced significant shifts in status, including periods of destruction and subsequent rebuilding. In later developments, the clan's influence grows significantly, with some predicting they may eventually become the sole remaining name in [[Sacred Valley]]. The clan is also associated with the [[Twin Star Sect]], which flies banners over [[Wei territory]].}}

== Unsouled ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|Founded by [[Elder Whisper]] and the clan founders, the Wei clan is a massive population of over one hundred thousand people in [[Sacred Valley]]. Operating under the motto 'Honor by any means,' the clan was built upon the contradiction that deception can serve honor, utilizing a path of light and dream madra to mislead their enemies. Their territory is characterized by purple and white buildings that reflect the colors of the orus tree and the snowfox, a creature represented by a five-tailed white fox on their banners. Under the leadership of [[Wei Jin Sairus]], the clan maintains alliances with the [[Heaven's Glory]] School and negotiates trade rights with the [[Kazan]]. Members of the clan engage in daily morning meditation, celebrate milestones like [[Kelsa]]'s advancement to [[Iron]], and participate in traditions such as children swimming in the [[Dragon River]].}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|The [[Wei clan]] uses a [[White Fox]] as its symbol.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c2">''Unsouled'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|The [[Wei clan]] accounts for over a hundred thousand of the people in [[Sacred Valley]].<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c2" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|The [[Wei clan]] is negotiating trade rights with the [[Kazan]].<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c3">''Unsouled'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|The [[Wei clan]]'s motto is 'Honor by any means'.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c3" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|The [[Path]] of the [[Wei clan]] uses [[madra]] of light and dreams to deceive enemies.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c3" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|The [[Wei clan]] was founded on the contradiction that deception can be used to serve honor.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c3" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|Most buildings in the [[Wei clan]] are purple and white.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c3" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|The colors of the [[Wei clan]] buildings reflect the purple leaves of the orus tree and the white fur of the snowfox.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c3" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|The [[Wei clan]] was created by [[Elder Whisper]] and the clan founders.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c3" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|The [[Wei clan]] members meditate and cycle every morning.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c4">''Unsouled'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|The most prominent families of the [[Wei clan]] gather before the [[Hall of Elders]].<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c5">''Unsouled'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|The [[Wei clan]] celebrated [[Kelsa]]'s advancement to [[Iron]].<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c7">''Unsouled'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|The Wei are identified by a white fox with five tails on their banners and always match white with purple.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c8">''Unsouled'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|The [[Wei clan]] feels pride watching the coordinated masterpiece of [[White Fox]] [[madra]].<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c8" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|The [[Wei clan]] has outdone their rivals in hospitality.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c8" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|The [[Wei clan]] wants a broad audience for the [[Seven-Year Festival]].<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c9">''Unsouled'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|The [[Wei clan]] is an ally of the [[Heaven's Glory]] School.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c10">''Unsouled'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Wei Jin Sairus]] leads the [[Wei clan]].<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c10" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|The Wei are a clan that has children who learn to swim in the [[Dragon River]].<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c11">''Unsouled'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|The [[Wei clan]] has a treasure hall.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c12">''Unsouled'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|The [[Wei clan]] has elders who reward killing [[Kazan]] [[Irons]].<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c17">''Unsouled'', chapter 17</ref>}}

== Soulsmith ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|Located within [[Sacred Valley]], the Wei clan is a group to which [[Lindon]] belongs. Led by their Patriarch, the clan specializes in the use of illusions and utilizes specific types of wardrobes. Within their social structure, the clan maintains a particular way of treating those who are [[Unsouled]].}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|The [[Wei clan]] is a clan that [[Lindon]] belongs to.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c2">''Soulsmith'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|The Patriarch of the [[Wei clan]] is a leader within the clan.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c5">''Soulsmith'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|The [[Wei clan]] treats [[Unsouled]] in a certain way.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c6">''Soulsmith'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|The [[Wei clan]] specializes in illusions.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c10">''Soulsmith'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|The [[Wei clan]] is located in [[Sacred Valley]].<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c16">''Soulsmith'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|The [[Wei clan]] uses specific types of wardrobes.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c18">''Soulsmith'', chapter 18</ref>}}

== Blackflame ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|The Wei clan utilizes simple exercises to train their [[Unsouled]] members. Their spiritual practices center around a [[Path]] of dreams and light, overseen by a Patriarch. Before any member learns their first [[Foundation technique]], they are required to learn how to read. During various holidays and festivals, the clan celebrates by shining colored lights into the night sky.}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|The [[Wei clan]] has simple exercises for [[Unsouled]].<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c3">''Blackflame'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|The [[Wei clan]] practices a [[Path]] of dreams and light.<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c6">''Blackflame'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|The [[Wei clan]] has a Patriarch.<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c10">''Blackflame'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|In the [[Wei clan]], everyone learns to read before learning their first [[Foundation technique]].<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c12">''Blackflame'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|The [[Wei clan]] shines colored lights into the night sky during festivals and holidays.<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c17">''Blackflame'', chapter 17</ref>}}

== Uncrowned ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|The [[Wei clan]] provided [[Lindon]] with space.<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c3">''Uncrowned'', chapter 3</ref>}}

== Bloodline ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|Located northeast of the valley, the Wei clan is a group that contains members known as [[Irons]] who wear copper or iron badges. The clan's territory is protected by thirty-foot walls and recently updated gates featuring decorated doors for the [[Seven-Year Festival]], while a massive chain of scripted goldsteel reinforces their boundary fields. Although the clan has exiles and members with unique deep brown hair, they were recently forced into action by their elders when a [[Dreadgod]] waded into their lands. Following the devastation, [[Lindon]] wondered if a [[Titan]] had crushed the clan to rubble and expressed a desire to return to them once he possessed great power.}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Wei clan]] has exiles.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c6">''Bloodline'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The person mentioned was an exile of the [[Wei clan]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c6" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Wei clan]] is located northeast of the valley.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c7">''Bloodline'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Seisha]]'s hair is a deep brown unique to the clan.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c8">''Bloodline'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Wei clan]] is a group [[Lindon]] plans to visit.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c9">''Bloodline'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Wei clan]] has walls that are about thirty feet tall.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c11">''Bloodline'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Wei clan]] territory has gates that were recently changed with freshly carved and decorated doors for the last [[Seven-Year Festival]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c11" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Wei clan]] contains members known as [[Irons]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c11" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Wei clan]] members wear iron or copper badges.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c11" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Wei clan]] is a clan.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c11" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Wei clan]] was whipped into action by its elders.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c12">''Bloodline'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Wei clan]] uses a massive chain of scripted goldsteel to reinforce boundary fields.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c12" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Wei clan]] put up a boundary field around [[Lindon]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c12" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Lindon]] wonders if the [[Titan]] crushed the [[Wei clan]] to rubble.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c15">''Bloodline'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Dreadgod]] waded into the [[Wei clan]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c17">''Bloodline'', chapter 17</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Lindon]] wanted to return to the [[Wei clan]] with great power.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c18">''Bloodline'', chapter 18</ref>}}

== Reaper ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Lindon]] grew up in the [[Wei clan]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c5">''Reaper'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The [[Wei clan]] has [[Jades]] that can coordinate for festival displays.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c5" />}}

== Dreadgod ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|Several members of the [[Wei clan]] have reached [[Lowgold]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c15">''Dreadgod'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Windfall]] rests on the former site of the [[Wei clan]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c21">''Dreadgod'', chapter 21</ref>}}

== Waybound ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The former site of the [[Wei clan]] was covered in white and purple wreckage.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c6">''Waybound'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Li Markuth]] challenges the [[Wei clan]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c35">''Waybound'', chapter 35</ref>}}

== Threshold: Stories from Cradle ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|The [[Wei clan]]'s new [[First Elder]] is the sister of the previous Patriarch.<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c8">''Threshold: Stories from Cradle'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|The [[Wei clan]] uses purple-and-white symbols.<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c8" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|The [[Wei clan]] has a reputation for underhanded tactics.<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c8" />}}

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|“Honor by any means,” the boys recited at once.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c3" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|“Call the elders and break out the wine,” Seisha said. “The Wei clan has a new Iron.”<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c7" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|“You’re…aren’t you…exiles from the Wei clan?”<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c6" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|"hers was still a deep brown like no one else in the clan."<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c8" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|“Yerin and I can go to the Wei clan,” Lindon said as he rolled up the map.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c9" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

[[Category:Groups]]
[[Category:Unsouled]]
[[Category:Soulsmith]]
[[Category:Blackflame]]
[[Category:Uncrowned]]
[[Category:Bloodline]]
[[Category:Reaper]]
[[Category:Dreadgod]]
[[Category:Waybound]]
[[Category:Threshold: Stories from Cradle]]
[[Category:Cradle]]
[[Category:Cradle groups]]
